description: This file defines the Balanda (Njo Viri) alphabet, pronunciation rules, special letters, vowel distinctions, consonant clusters, and orthographic constraints. All letters and clusters are phonemic and must be preserved exactly as written.

alphabet_system.structure: {"script": "Latin-based", "status": "phonemic", "principle": "Each letter or cluster represents a meaningful sound contrast"}
alphabet_system.notes: Latin-based | phonemic | Each letter or cluster represents a meaningful sound contrast

core_letters.notes: A; E; I; Ï; O; Ö; U; Ü | B; D; F; G; H; J; K; L; M; N; ŋ; P; R; S; T; V; W; Y; Z

special_letters.structure: {"ŋ": {"type": "nasal consonant", "pronunciation": "/ŋ/", "example": "ŋo (you)", "constraint": "Must never be replaced with 'ng'"}, "Ï": {"type": "vowel", "pronunciation": "/ï/", "example": "Lï (dream)", "constraint": "Distinct from I"}, "Ö": {"type": "vowel", "pronunciation": "/ö/", "example": "Völö (stubborn)", "constraint": "Distinct from O"}, "Ü": {"type": "vowel", "pronunciation": "/ü/", "example": "Ülü (dancer)", "constraint": "Distinct from U"}}

capitalization.notes: Capitalization errors are treated as spelling errors

orthographic_constraints.structure: Letters with diacritics (Ï, Ö, Ü, ŋ) must never be substituted; Consonant clusters must not be split or simplified; Phonemic spelling must be preserved even if unfamiliar to English; Loanword spelling must conform to Balanda phonology; Lowercase and uppercase errors are orthographic errors; ŋ is a letter, not a digraph

validation_rules.structure: {"valid_letter": "Must be listed in the Balanda alphabet", "valid_cluster": "Must be one of the recognized phonemic clusters", "invalid_forms": ["ngo → must be ŋo", "ulu → must be Ülü"]}
validation_rules.notes: Must be listed in the Balanda alphabet | Must be one of the recognized phonemic clusters | ngo → must be ŋo; ulu → must be Ülü

output: Letter F, pronounced /fe/, example: Fulu (thank, praise)
Letter N, pronounced /ne/, example: Nokoro (to wrinkle)
Letter U, pronounced /u/, example: Udoo (male name)
Letter Ü, pronounced /ü/, example: Ülü (dancer, male)
Balanda (Njo Viri) uses a Latin-based alphabet with additional characters such as ŋ, Ï, Ö, and Ü. These letters are phonemic and must not be replaced.
